name: dnswalk
title: dnswalk
summary: Checks dns zone information using nameserver lookups
description:
Checks dns zone information using nameserver lookups
dnswalk is a DNS debugger.  It performs zone transfers of specified
domains, and checks the database in numerous ways for internal
consistency, as well as accuracy.

arguments.description: Turn on warning of duplicate A records. (see below)
arguments.raw: -a  Turn on warning of duplicate A records. (see below)

arguments.description: Print debugging and 'status' information to stderr. (Use only if redirecting stdout)
arguments.raw: -d  Print debugging and 'status' information to stderr. (Use only if redirecting stdout)

arguments.description: Suppress check for invalid characters in a domain name. (see below)
arguments.raw: -i  Suppress check for invalid characters in a domain name. (see below)

arguments.description: Perform "lame delegation" checking. For every NS record, check to see that the listed
arguments.raw: -l  Perform "lame delegation" checking. For every NS record, check to see that the listed

arguments.description: Perform checks only if the zone has been modified since the previous run.
arguments.raw: -m  Perform checks only if the zone has been modified since the previous run.

arguments.description: Recursively descend sub-domains of the specified domain. Use with care.
arguments.raw: -r  Recursively descend sub-domains of the specified domain. Use with care.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dnswalk because Checks dns zone information using nameserver lookups.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dnswalk as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnswalk [ -adilrfFm ] domain.` as documented upstream for dnswalk.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dnswalk with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dnswalk --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install dnswalk. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dnswalk [ -adilrfFm ] domain.`. Checks dns zone information using nameserver lookups.
